review:
This game is absolute ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥. Every shot you take is a coinflip, probability is a lie. You will miss a point blank 99% shot, but the yolo 15% shot you take will hit and kill. Still not too sure if XCom was recruiting Storm Troopers or not.

The aliens are also complete jerks. Sectopods are an actual crime on humanity itself, I hope to god you got bluescreen rounds by the time this game throws multiple at you at a time. Sometimes aliens will appear out of nowhere and screw your player phase up. It's almost unfair how out of control this game is.

This game is a massive test of patience. You will be pissed off, you will be angry, but at least you can be glad your terrible luck is not going towards financial ruin. And I love it. I only have as many hours as I do because I'm busy with college and I know I will get addicted if I even so much as touch this game.

Every choice feels like it matters, that's what makes XCom feel more than a strategy game, it is a war game. The fate of your soldiers, how easy the rest of the game will be, the fate of the planet, everything depends on your choices, and I love it. It makes me feel more invested towards building a strong cast of soldiers, and the reward feels just as great. 

There are obviously some complaints I have. It's frusterating when a whole horde of enemies just appear out of nowhere when you try to advance, the game can be unoptimized at times, and modding the game itself is just hell. All of the amazing mods you can use make this game worth it alone. But getting them all to run is a nightmare, especially because the offical launchers are trash, always use the alternative one made by fans, trust me. 

It is 100% not going to be a game for everyone. If you're a casual gamer that does not like it when a game throws unfair BS at you, this game will be hard to get through. But it also has to be, you're leading a resistance that wants to take earth back from the aliens. Don't feel ashamed by save scumming, we have all done it, and it does not fix all your problems, it still leaves a puzzle you have to figure out when it rerolls events.

Easily recommend this to anyone who loves strategy games like myself. The game usually goes on sale for dirt cheap, so nab it and War of The Chosen if you can. Although I reccomend playing vanilla before War of the Chosen first. Both are very fun.

Also relist all the DLC seperatly again 2K, come on.

review:
This is one of the best turn-based strategy games I have ever played. It is also one of the hardest. The theme is very interesting. Earth has already lost the war. Aliens rule the world, and you lead a small resistance from a hidden base.
The difficulty is no joke. Every move counts. You will miss shots you thought were guaranteed hits. Your best soldiers can die forever because of one bad decision. Mission timers force you to take risks. The Avatar Project is always moving forward. The pressure never stops.
What I like about XCOM 2 is that you never know what will happen next. A simple task can turn into a disaster in two turns. 
TLDR; I would recommend the game to anyone who likes turn-based strategy games and doesn’t mind a steep learning curve.

[h2] Pros: [/h2]
- Hard but fair
- Adjustable difficulty
- Great progression system
- Interesting theme
- Great soundtrack
- High replayability and great mod support

[h2] Cons: [/h2]
- Steep learning curve

[h1] Overall rating: 9/10 [/h1]

Znakomita pozycja. W mojej ocenie zdecydowanie lepsza od jedynki. Wraz z dodatkiem War of the Chosen dostajemy po prostu więcej ciekawszej i wymagającej zawartości. Dzięki temu gra stała się też trudniejsza, a to z kolei wymusza rozważne planowanie naszych działań. 
Nie będę się rozpisywał. Jeśli komuś spodobała się jedynka to tym bardziej powinien sięgnąć po kontynuację i to najlepiej ze wszystkimi dodatkami. Must have dla fanów taktycznych turówek i ufoków.

Pero siempre vuelves a recaer, este juego no se que tiene pero siempre tiene te dan ganas de volver y probar una build nueva y una campaña nueva con mods, mas si juegas Long War. Creo que es porque es  muy satisfactorio de jugar cuando te salen las cosas bien, matar un pod de 3 aliens tochos sin que te toquen se siente igual de bien que apalizar a un sionista por la calle.

But you always fall back, I don't know what this game has but it always makes you want to go back and try a new build and a new campaign with mods, especially if you play Long War. I think it's because it's very satisfying to play when things go well, killing a pod of 3 aliens without them touching you feels just as good as beating up a Zionist in the street.

review: This is easily in my top 2 favourite games of all time. I bought a PC just so I could mod this game. Now even playing just the base game i have had so many memorable moments, so many characters who miss a 98% shot just to get domed and we lose but then the next mission someone worse hits a 2% shot and it is the most satisfying things on this planet. But then once you have finished a vanilla campaign, you can then move on to the real fun the mods. I have over 1000 mods and the game takes forever to load however, the amount of gameplay features, customisation, new maps, new music just all around new fun you may aswell call this xcom 3. This game i can not big up enough.

No more relying on rangers to carry the banner in the early stage
